title: Section 16
content: 1[(1)] If the indorser signs his name only, the indorsement is said to be "in blank," and if he adds a direction to pay the amount mentioned in the instrument to, or to the order of, a specified person, the indorsement is said to be "in full", and the person so specified "Indorsee".--is called the “indorsee” of the instrument. 2[(2) The provisions of this Act relating to a payee shall apply with the necessary modifications to an indorsee.]
footnote: 1. S. 16 renumbered as sub-section (1) by s. 3, ibid. 2. Ins. by Act 5 of 1914, s. 3.
